name: createApp()
description: Returns an application instance with Express-like routing, middleware support, WebSocket handling, settings management, error handling, and an HTTP/HTTPS server. All HTTP method shortcuts return the App instance for chaining.

methods.description: Register global middleware; fn(req, res, next).
Register path-scoped middleware or mount a Router sub-app. The prefix is stripped from req.url.
Register GET route handlers. Returns App (chainable). With 1 arg, acts as settings getter — see set().
Register POST route handlers. Returns App (chainable).
Register PUT route handlers. Returns App (chainable).
Register DELETE route handlers. Returns App (chainable).
Register PATCH route handlers. Returns App (chainable).
Register OPTIONS route handlers. Returns App (chainable).
Register HEAD route handlers. Returns App (chainable).
Register handlers for ALL HTTP methods. Returns App (chainable).
Dual-purpose: 1 arg = get setting value, 2 args = set setting value. Setter returns App (chainable).
Set a boolean setting to true. Returns App (chainable).
Set a boolean setting to false. Returns App (chainable).
Check if a setting is truthy. Returns boolean.
Check if a setting is falsy. Returns boolean.
Shared key-value store merged into every req.locals and res.locals per request.
Register a handler for a named route parameter. fn(req, res, next, value). Returns App (chainable).
Create a route group under a prefix with shared middleware. Last arg is (router) => {} callback. Returns App.
Returns a chainable route object with .get(), .post(), etc. for a single path. Alias for router.route().
Register a WebSocket upgrade handler. handler receives (ws, req).
Register a global error handler: fn(err, req, res, next). Only one at a time.
Start HTTP server (default port 3000), or HTTPS if tlsOpts includes { key, cert }. Returns the server instance.
Gracefully close the server and invoke the optional callback.
Return a flat array of all registered routes (including WS) for introspection.
Bound request handler suitable for http.createServer(app.handler).

name: Router()
description: Creates a standalone router for modular route grouping. Supports all HTTP method helpers, route chaining via .route(), sub-router mounting via .use(), protocol-aware { secure } matching, and introspection via .routes().

methods.description: Register GET route. Optional { secure } option.
Register POST route.
Register PUT route.
Register DELETE route.
Register PATCH route.
Register OPTIONS route.
Register HEAD route.
Register route for all HTTP methods.
Register middleware or mount a nested sub-router.
Return a chainable object with .get(), .post(), etc. for a single path.
Return an array of all registered routes with method, path, and secure flag.

name: Request (req)
description: Wraps the incoming Node http.IncomingMessage with convenient properties and helpers. Includes HTTPS detection, cookie access, content negotiation, and Express-compatible properties like originalUrl, baseUrl, hostname, and xhr.
example:
app.get('/info', (req, res) => {
	console.log(req.method)           // 'GET'
	console.log(req.url)              // '/info?foo=bar'
	console.log(req.originalUrl)      // '/api/info?foo=bar' (full original)
	console.log(req.baseUrl)          // '/api' (set by mounted routers)
	console.log(req.path)             // '/info'
	console.log(req.query)            // { foo: 'bar' }
	console.log(req.params)           // { id: '42' } (from route pattern)
	console.log(req.ip)               // '127.0.0.1'
	console.log(req.secure)           // true/false
	console.log(req.protocol)         // 'https' or 'http'
	console.log(req.hostname)         // 'example.com'
	console.log(req.xhr)              // true if X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest
	console.log(req.fresh)            // conditional GET freshness
	console.log(req.stale)            // !req.fresh
	console.log(req.app)              // the App instance
	console.log(req.cookies)          // {} (populated by cookieParser)
	console.log(req.locals)           // {} request-scoped store
	console.log(req.get('host'))      // 'localhost:3000'
	console.log(req.is('json'))       // false
	console.log(req.accepts('json', 'html'))  // 'json'
	console.log(req.subdomains())     // ['api'] for api.example.com
	console.log(req.range(1000))      // { type: 'bytes', ranges: [{start:0,end:499}] }
	res.json({ ok: true })
})

methods.description: HTTP method (GET, POST, etc.).
Request URL path + query string (may be rewritten by mounted routers).
The original request URL before any router rewrites. Never changes.
The prefix path where the router was mounted. Empty string for root app.
URL pathname without query string.
Raw request headers (lowercased keys).
Parsed query string via URLSearchParams.
Route parameters populated by the router (e.g. { id: '42' }).
Parsed request body (null until a body parser runs).
Remote IP address from req.socket.remoteAddress.
true when the connection is over TLS (HTTPS).
'https' or 'http' based on connection type.
Hostname from x-forwarded-host or host header, port stripped.
true for GET/HEAD when If-None-Match / If-Modified-Since match cached version.
Inverse of fresh — true when the response needs to be sent in full.
true when X-Requested-With header equals 'XMLHttpRequest'.
Parsed cookies (populated by cookieParser middleware).
Verified signed cookies (populated by cookieParser with a secret).
Request-scoped store. Merged with app.locals on each request.
Reference to the App instance handling this request.
The underlying http.IncomingMessage.
Get a request header (case-insensitive).
Check if Content-Type contains the given type string (e.g. 'json', 'text/html').
Content negotiation — returns the best match from the given types based on Accept header, or false. Supports shorthands: 'json', 'html', 'text', 'xml', 'css', 'js'.
Returns subdomain array. Default offset=2 strips TLD+domain. E.g. 'api.blog.example.com' → ['blog', 'api'].
Parse Range header. Returns { type, ranges: [{start, end}] }, -1 (unsatisfiable), or -2 (malformed/missing).

name: Response (res)
description: Wraps the outgoing Node http.ServerResponse with chainable helpers for setting status, headers, cookies, sending responses, content negotiation, file streaming, and opening an SSE stream.

methods.description: Set HTTP status code. Returns this (chainable).
Set status code and send the status text as the response body (e.g. 404 → 'Not Found').
Set a response header. CRLF injection protection built-in. Returns this (chainable).
Get a previously-set response header (case-insensitive).
Append to a response header (comma-separated). CRLF injection protection. Returns this (chainable).
Add a field to the Vary header. Deduplicates and handles '*'. Returns this (chainable).
Set Content-Type. Accepts shorthands: 'json', 'html', 'text', 'xml', 'form', 'bin'. Chainable.
Set Link header from an object. E.g. links({ next: '/p/2', last: '/p/5' }) → '<\/p/2>; rel="next", <\/p/5>; rel="last"'. Chainable.
Set the Location header. Returns this (chainable).
true once headers have been flushed to the client (delegates to raw response).
Reference to the App instance handling this request.
Response-scoped store. Merged with app.locals on each request.
Send the response. Auto-detects Content-Type: Buffer→octet-stream, '<…'→html, string→text, object→json.
Set Content-Type to application/json and send the object.
Set Content-Type to text/plain and send the string.
Set Content-Type to text/html and send the string.
Redirect to URL. Default status is 302.
Content negotiation — takes an object mapping MIME types to handler functions. Supports 'default' key. Returns 406 if no match.
Stream a file as the response. Auto-detects Content-Type. opts: { root, headers }. Path traversal protection built-in.
Set Content-Disposition: attachment and stream the file. Optional filename overrides the download name.

Set a cookie. Object values are auto-serialised as JSON cookies (j: prefix). opts: path('/'), httpOnly(true), sameSite('Lax'), domain, maxAge(seconds), expires, secure, signed(false), priority, partitioned. Returns this (chainable).
Clear a cookie by setting expires to epoch. Returns this (chainable).
Open a Server-Sent Events stream. Returns an SSEStream controller with send(), event(), comment(), close(), etc.

name: app.ws(path, [opts], handler)
description: Register a WebSocket upgrade handler on the application. Uses RFC 6455 framing with built-in ping/pong, sub-protocol negotiation, and client verification. The handler receives (ws, req) where ws is a WebSocketConnection with a rich event-driven API.

methods.description: Send a text or binary message. opts.binary forces a binary frame.
JSON-serialize and send as a text frame.
Send a ping frame (auto-pings are built-in via pingInterval).
Send a pong frame.
Initiate a graceful close with optional status code and reason.
Forcefully destroy the underlying socket.
Listen for events: 'message', 'close', 'error', 'ping', 'pong', 'drain'.
One-time event listener.
Remove a specific event listener.
Remove all listeners for an event, or all events.
Return the number of listeners for an event.

options.notes: Maximum incoming frame size in bytes. Frames exceeding this close the connection with 1009.
Auto-ping interval in ms. Set to 0 to disable.
fn(req) → boolean. Return false to reject the upgrade with 403 Forbidden.

name: WebSocketConnection
description: Represents a single WebSocket connection. Created automatically when a client upgrades to WebSocket at a registered ws() path. Provides an event-driven API with per-connection data store, connection metadata, and binary/text messaging. Exposes static state constants: CONNECTING (0), OPEN (1), CLOSING (2), CLOSED (3).

methods.description: Unique connection identifier (e.g. ws_1_l8x3k).
0=CONNECTING, 1=OPEN, 2=CLOSING, 3=CLOSED (mirrors the WebSocket spec).
Negotiated sub-protocol from the Sec-WebSocket-Protocol handshake.
Headers from the initial HTTP upgrade request.
Remote IP address of the WebSocket client.
Parsed query parameters from the upgrade URL.
Full upgrade request URL.
true when the connection is over TLS (WSS).
Requested WebSocket extensions header from the client.
Maximum payload size in bytes for this connection.
Timestamp (ms) when the connection was established.
Milliseconds since the connection opened (computed getter).
Number of bytes queued for transmission.
Arbitrary per-connection data store. Use ws.data.key = value for user data.

name: WebSocketPool
description: Connection and room manager for WebSocket connections. Provides broadcast, room-based messaging, and connection lifecycle management. Auto-removes connections on close. All mutating methods return the pool for chaining.

methods.description: Add a connection to the pool. Auto-removes on close. Returns this.
Remove a connection from the pool and all its rooms. Returns this.
Join a connection to a named room. Returns this.
Remove a connection from a room. Cleans up empty rooms. Returns this.
Send a message to ALL connected clients. Optional exclude connection (e.g. the sender).
JSON-serialize and broadcast to all clients.
Send a message to all connections in a specific room.
JSON-serialize and send to all connections in a room.
Get an array of all connections in a room.
Get all room names a connection belongs to.
Total number of active connections (getter).
Number of connections in a specific room.
List of all active room names (getter).
Array of all active connections (getter).
Close all connections gracefully. Default code=1001, reason='Server shutdown'.

name: res.sse([opts]) / SSEStream
description: Opens a Server-Sent Events stream from a route handler. Returns an SSEStream controller with methods for sending events, comments, and controlling the stream lifecycle. Supports auto-incrementing IDs, keep-alive, retry hints, and per-stream data store.

methods.description: Send unnamed data event. Objects are auto-JSON-serialized.
Alias for send() with an object.
Send a named event (event: name\ndata: ...).
Send a comment line (: text). Useful for keep-alive or debugging.
Send a retry: ms hint to set the client's reconnection interval.
Start or restart a keep-alive timer that sends periodic comments.
Flush buffered data through proxies (writes 2 KB padding).
Close the SSE stream from the server side.
Listen for 'close' or 'error' events.
One-time event listener.
Remove a specific event listener.
Remove all listeners for an event, or all events.
Return the number of listeners for an event.

options.notes: Reconnection interval hint (ms) sent to the client on stream open.
Auto keep-alive comment interval in ms. 0 = disabled.
Comment text sent by the keep-alive timer.
Auto-increment event IDs starting from startId.
Starting value for auto-generated IDs.
Bytes of initial padding (helps flush proxy buffers on connect).
HTTP status code for the SSE response.
Additional response headers merged into the SSE response.

name: helmet([opts])
description: Security headers middleware that sets sensible defaults for Content-Security-Policy, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, and more. All 16 options can be individually overridden or disabled by setting them to false.

options.notes: CSP directive object. Keys: defaultSrc, scriptSrc, styleSrc, imgSrc, fontSrc, etc. Set false to disable.
Set true to send Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy: require-corp.
Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy value. Set false to disable.
Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy value. Set false to disable.
Set X-DNS-Prefetch-Control: off. Set false to skip.
X-Frame-Options value: 'deny' or 'sameorigin'. Set false to disable.
Removes the X-Powered-By header. Set false to keep it.
Enable Strict-Transport-Security. Set false to disable.
HSTS max-age in seconds.
Include subdomains in HSTS policy.
Add preload flag for HSTS preload list eligibility.
Set X-Download-Options: noopen. Set false to skip.
Set X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff. Set false to skip.
X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies value. Set false to disable.
Referrer-Policy value. Set false to disable.
Set true to enable X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block.

name: timeout(ms, [opts])
description: Request timeout middleware. If the handler doesn't respond within the given time, the request is terminated with a configurable status code and message. Sets req.timedOut boolean for downstream checks.

options.notes: Timeout duration in milliseconds. Can also be passed as the first argument.
HTTP status code sent when the timeout fires.
Response body text sent on timeout.

name: requestId([opts])
description: Generates a unique request ID for every incoming request. Sets req.id and mirrors it as a response header for request tracing and log correlation. Uses crypto.randomUUID() by default.

options.notes: Header name used to read (if trustProxy) and write the request ID.
Custom function returning a unique ID string.
When true, trusts the incoming header value (max 128 chars) instead of generating a new one.

name: cookieParser([secret], [opts])
description: Parses the Cookie header and populates req.cookies. When a secret is provided, signed cookies (prefixed with 's:') are verified with timing-safe HMAC-SHA256 and placed in req.signedCookies. JSON cookies (prefixed with 'j:') are auto-parsed. Exposes req.secret and req.secrets for downstream middleware. Static helpers: sign, unsign, jsonCookie, parseJSON.

methods.description: Static method. Signs a value with HMAC-SHA256. Returns 's:<value>.<signature>'.
Static method. Verifies and unsigns a signed cookie value. Secret can be a string or array (rotation). Returns the original value or false.
Static method. Serialises any value as a JSON cookie string prefixed with 'j:'.
Static method. Parses a 'j:'-prefixed JSON cookie string. Returns parsed value or original string.

options.notes: Signing secret(s) for cookie verification. Array enables key rotation — first key signs, all keys verify. Exposed as req.secret and req.secrets.
When true, decodeURIComponent is applied to cookie values.

name: csrf([opts])
description: CSRF protection middleware using the double-submit cookie pattern. Generates a cryptographic token on every state-changing request and validates it on subsequent mutations. Token is set as an HttpOnly cookie and must be sent back via header, body field, or query parameter.

options.notes: Name of the cookie used to store the CSRF secret.
Header name checked for the CSRF token.
Length of the random salt used in token generation.
HMAC secret for token signing. Auto-generated per process if not provided.
HTTP methods that skip CSRF validation.
URL paths that skip CSRF validation (e.g. webhook endpoints).
Custom error handler: fn(err, req, res). Called when token is missing or invalid.

name: validate(schema, [opts])
description: Request validation middleware. Validates and coerces req.body, req.query, and req.params against a schema. Supports 11 data types with automatic type coercion, pattern matching, range checks, enum validation, and custom validators. Unknown fields are stripped by default (mass-assignment protection).

methods.description: Static helper. Validate and coerce a single value. Returns { value, error }.
Static helper. Validate an entire object against a schema. Returns { sanitized, errors }.

options.notes: Remove fields not defined in the schema (prevents mass-assignment attacks).
Custom error handler: fn(errors, req, res, next). errors is an array of error strings.

name: env
description: Typed environment configuration. Loads and validates environment variables from .env files with schema enforcement, type coercion, and sensible defaults. Proxy-based — access variables as env.KEY, env('KEY'), or env.get('KEY'). Supports multi-file loading with environment-specific overrides.

methods.description: Get an environment variable. Proxy-based — use either function call or property access.
Load .env files, apply schema validation & type coercion. opts: { path, override }.
Get a variable from the loaded store or process.env.
Get a variable; throws if missing or empty.
Check if a variable exists. Returns boolean.
Return a shallow copy of all loaded variables.
Clear the loaded store and schema. Useful for testing.
Parse a .env-format string into a key-value object.

options.notes: Directory to look for .env files.
When true, .env values override existing process.env values.

name: Database
description: Lightweight ORM entry point. Connect to a database, register models, sync schemas, and run queries. Supports 6 adapters: memory (in-process), json (file-based), sqlite, mysql, postgres, and mongo. All CRUD operations are async and adapter-agnostic.

methods.description: Static. Connect to a database. type: 'memory', 'json', 'sqlite', 'mysql', 'postgres', 'mongo'. Returns Database instance.
Register a Model class with this database. Returns this (chainable).
Register multiple Model classes at once. Returns this (chainable).
Async. Create/update tables for all registered models.
Async. Drop tables for all registered models (reverse order).
Async. Close the database connection.
Retrieve a registered model class by table name.
Async. Execute callback within a transaction — rolls back on error, commits on success. Adapters without native transactions run the callback directly.

options.notes: Adapter type: 'memory' | 'json' | 'sqlite' | 'mysql' | 'postgres' | 'mongo'.
File path for 'json' adapter.
Database file for 'sqlite' adapter.
Hostname for mysql/postgres/mongo.
Port for mysql/postgres/mongo.
Database name for mysql/postgres/mongo.
Auth credentials for mysql/postgres/mongo.

name: Model
description: Base class for ORM models. Extend it to define your schema, table, timestamps, soft-deletes, hooks, scopes, hidden fields, and relationships. Provides static CRUD, upsert, exists, scoped queries, instance lifecycle methods, increment/decrement, a fluent query builder, and eager-loading including many-to-many.

methods.description: Async. Insert a record and return a Model instance.
Async. Insert multiple records. Returns array of Model instances.
Async. Find all matching records. No conditions = find all.
Async. Find the first matching record or null.
Async. Find by primary key or null.
Async. Find or create. Returns { instance, created: boolean }.
Async. Insert or update — finds by conditions, updates if exists, creates otherwise. Returns { instance, created: boolean }.
Async. Returns true if any matching records exist.
Async. Bulk update matching records. Returns affected count.
Async. Bulk delete matching records. Returns affected count.
Async. Count matching records.
Returns a Query builder for fluent querying.
Start a query with a named scope applied. Scopes are defined in static scopes = {}.
Async. Find the first record. Returns Promise<Model|null>.
Async. Find the last record (by PK descending). Returns Promise<Model|null>.
Async. Get all records (alias for find). Returns Promise<Model[]>.
Async. Rich pagination: returns { data, total, page, perPage, pages, hasNext, hasPrev }.
Async. Process all records in batches. fn(batch, batchIndex) — supports async.
Async. Get a random record. Returns Promise<Model|null>.
Async. Pluck values for a single column. Returns Promise<Array>.
Define a one-to-many relationship.
Define a one-to-one relationship.
Define an inverse one-to-one/many relationship.
Define a many-to-many relationship via junction table. opts: { through, foreignKey, otherKey, localKey, relatedKey }.
Async. Insert or update the record (upsert).
Async. Update specific fields. Returns this.
Async. Delete the record (soft-delete if enabled).
Async. Restore a soft-deleted record. Returns this.
Async. Re-fetch the record from the database. Returns this.
Async. Increment a numeric field. Updates DB and instance.
Async. Decrement a numeric field. Updates DB and instance.
Return a plain object copy of the record. Respects static hidden = [] to exclude sensitive fields.
Async. Eager-load a related model or collection. Supports hasMany, hasOne, belongsTo, and belongsToMany.

options.notes: Static property. Database table name.
Static property. Column definitions with type, constraints, and defaults.
Static property. Automatically add createdAt / updatedAt columns.
Static property. Add deletedAt column; delete() sets it instead of removing.
Static property. Lifecycle hooks: beforeCreate, afterCreate, beforeUpdate, afterUpdate, beforeDelete, afterDelete.
Static property. Fields excluded from toJSON() — e.g. ['password', 'resetToken'].
Static property. Named query scopes — functions that receive a Query and return it. Called via Model.scope(name, ...args).

name: Query
description: Fluent query builder returned by Model.query(). All filter/sort/limit methods are chainable. Execute with .exec(), .first(), .count(), .exists(), .pluck(), or aggregates. Also thenable — you can await the query directly.

methods.description: Select specific columns. Chainable.
Return unique rows only. Chainable.
Add a WHERE condition. Supports object form, (field, value), or (field, op, value). Operators: =, !=, >, <, >=, <=, LIKE, IN, NOT IN, BETWEEN, IS NULL, IS NOT NULL.
Add an OR WHERE condition. Chainable.
WHERE field IS NULL. Chainable.
WHERE field IS NOT NULL. Chainable.
WHERE field IN (...values). Chainable.
WHERE field NOT IN (...values). Chainable.
WHERE field BETWEEN low AND high. Chainable.
WHERE field NOT BETWEEN low AND high. Chainable.
WHERE field LIKE pattern (% and _ wildcards). Chainable.
Sort results. dir: 'asc' (default) or 'desc'. Chainable.
Maximum number of results. Chainable.
Skip n records. Chainable.
Pagination helper. 1-indexed. perPage defaults to 20. Chainable.
Group results by columns. Chainable.
Add a HAVING condition (use with groupBy). Chainable.
INNER JOIN. Chainable.
LEFT JOIN. Chainable.
RIGHT JOIN. Chainable.
Include soft-deleted records in results. Chainable.
Apply a named scope from the model's static scopes. Chainable.
Execute the query. Returns Promise<Model[]>.
Execute and return the first result. Returns Promise<Model|null>.
Execute and return the count. Returns Promise<number>.
Returns Promise<boolean> — true if any matching records exist.
Returns Promise<Array> of values for a single column.
Returns Promise<number> — sum of a numeric column.
Returns Promise<number> — average of a numeric column.
Returns Promise<*> — minimum value of a column.
Returns Promise<*> — maximum value of a column.
Alias for limit() — LINQ naming. Chainable.
Alias for offset() — LINQ naming. Chainable.
Alias for exec() — returns Promise<Model[]>.
Shorthand for orderBy(field, 'desc'). Chainable.
Execute and return the last result. Returns Promise<Model|null>.
Conditionally apply query logic if condition is truthy. Chainable.
Conditionally apply query logic if condition is falsy. Chainable.
Inspect the query for debugging without breaking the chain. Chainable.
Process results in batches. Calls fn(batch, batchIndex) for each chunk.
Execute and iterate each result. fn(item, index) — supports async.
Execute, transform each result. Returns Promise<Array>.
Execute, post-filter results in JS. Returns Promise<Model[]>.
Execute and reduce results to a single value.
Rich pagination: returns { data, total, page, perPage, pages, hasNext, hasPrev }.
Inject raw SQL WHERE clause (SQL adapters only). Parameterized. Chainable.

name: TYPES
description: Column type constants for ORM schema definitions. Use these instead of raw strings for type safety and IDE autocomplete.

methods.description: Text column (varchar equivalent).
Integer column.
Floating-point number column.
Boolean column.
Date-only column.
Date+time column.
JSON column (stored as serialized object).
Long text column (no length limit).
Binary data column.
UUID string column.

name: compress([opts])
description: Response compression middleware using brotli, gzip, or deflate based on the client's Accept-Encoding header. Brotli is preferred when available (Node ≥ 11.7). Automatically negotiates encoding, respects threshold, skips SSE streams, and supports a filter function to skip specific requests.

options.notes: Minimum response body size in bytes before compression is applied.
Compression level (1–9). Higher = smaller output, more CPU.
fn(req, res) → boolean. Return false to skip compression for the request.

name: json([opts])
description: Parses JSON request bodies and populates req.body. Skips non-matching Content-Types and calls next(). Supports requireSecure to enforce HTTPS.

options.notes: Maximum body size. Accepts bytes or unit strings like '100kb', '1mb', '1gb'.
When true, only accepts objects and arrays (rejects primitives).
Optional function passed to JSON.parse for custom reviving.
MIME type to match. Supports wildcards ('*/*') or a custom predicate function.
When true, rejects non-HTTPS requests with 403 'HTTPS required'.

name: urlencoded([opts])
description: Parses application/x-www-form-urlencoded bodies into req.body. Supports requireSecure.

options.notes: When true, supports rich nested bracket syntax (a[b]=1, a[]=1). When false, returns flat key/value pairs.
Maximum body size (bytes or unit string).
MIME type to match.
When true, rejects non-HTTPS requests with 403 'HTTPS required'.

name: text([opts])
description: Reads plain text request bodies into req.body as a string. Supports requireSecure.

options.notes: MIME matcher — uses wildcard by default so it matches text/plain, text/html, etc.
Maximum body size.
Character encoding used to decode the incoming bytes.
When true, rejects non-HTTPS requests with 403 'HTTPS required'.

name: raw([opts])
description: Receives raw bytes as a Buffer on req.body. Useful for binary payloads. Supports requireSecure.

options.notes: MIME type to match for the raw parser.
Maximum body size. Returns 413 if exceeded.
When true, rejects non-HTTPS requests with 403 'HTTPS required'.

name: multipart([opts])
description: Streaming multipart parser that writes file parts to disk and collects text fields. Sets req.body to { fields, files }. Supports requireSecure.

options.notes: Directory to store uploaded files. Relative paths resolve from process.cwd(). Created automatically if missing.
Maximum file size in bytes. Exceeding this aborts the upload and returns HTTP 413.
When true, rejects non-HTTPS requests with 403 'HTTPS required'.

name: static(rootPath, [opts])
description: Serves static files from a filesystem directory with Content-Type detection (60+ MIME types), dotfile policy, caching, extension fallback, and custom header hooks. Only handles GET and HEAD requests.

options.notes: File to serve for directory requests. Set false to disable auto-index.
Cache-Control max-age in milliseconds. Converted to seconds internally.
'allow' | 'deny' | 'ignore'. deny→403, ignore→skip to next middleware, allow→serve normally.
Fallback extensions to try when a file is not found (e.g. ['html', 'htm']).
Hook (res, filePath) => {} to set custom headers before streaming the file.

name: cors([opts])
description: Small CORS middleware. Handles preflight OPTIONS requests automatically with 204 and sets standard CORS headers on all responses. Supports suffix-based origin matching for subdomains.

options.notes: Allowed origins. '*' allows any. Array entries starting with '.' do suffix matching. Set falsy to disable.
Allowed HTTP methods for Access-Control-Allow-Methods.
Allowed headers for Access-Control-Allow-Headers.
When true and a specific origin matches, sets Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true.

name: fetch(url, [opts])
description: Small Node HTTP/HTTPS client returning a response with status, ok, statusText, secure, url, headers.get(), and helpers: text(), json(), arrayBuffer(). Supports timeouts, AbortSignal, progress callbacks, auto-serialization, and TLS options passthrough for HTTPS URLs.

options.notes: HTTP method (uppercased internally).
Request headers.
Request body. Plain objects are auto-JSON-encoded. URLSearchParams produce urlencoded bodies. Streams are piped.
Request timeout in milliseconds. Creates an ETIMEOUT error on expiry.
AbortController signal to cancel the request.
Optional agent for connection pooling or proxies.
Callback receiving { loaded, total } on each response chunk.
Callback receiving { loaded, total } during body upload.
TLS option: set false to accept self-signed certs.
TLS options: custom certificate authority, client cert, key, or PKCS12 bundle.
Passphrase for the private key or pfx.
SNI hostname for TLS negotiation.
TLS version constraints (e.g. 'TLSv1.2', 'TLSv1.3').

name: rateLimit([opts])
description: In-memory, per-IP rate-limiting middleware. Sets standard X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset headers on every response. Returns 429 JSON error when the limit is exceeded. Expired entries are cleaned up automatically.

options.notes: Time window in milliseconds (default: 1 minute).
Maximum requests per window per key.
HTTP status code for rate-limited responses.

options.default: Too many requests, please try again later.
options.notes: Error message returned in the JSON response body when limit is exceeded.
Custom function to extract a rate-limit key from the request.
(req) => boolean. Return true to skip rate limiting for the request.
(req, res) => void. Custom handler for rate-limited requests instead of the default JSON response.

name: logger([opts])
description: Request-logging middleware that prints method, URL, status code, and response time. Supports three output formats with optional ANSI colorization. Hooks into the response 'finish' event to capture final status.

options.notes: 'dev' (colorized method/status/time), 'short' (IP + method + url + status + time), or 'tiny' (method + url + status + time).
Custom log output function.
Enable or disable ANSI color codes. Auto-detected from process.stdout.isTTY.

name: Error Classes
description: Built-in HTTP error classes with status codes, machine-readable codes, and optional details. Every error extends HttpError which carries statusCode, code, and details. Throw them in route handlers — the router catches and sends the correct HTTP response automatically.

methods.description: Base class. opts: { code, details }.
400 Bad Request.
401 Unauthorized.
403 Forbidden.
404 Not Found.
405 Method Not Allowed.
409 Conflict.
410 Gone.
413 Payload Too Large.
422 Unprocessable Entity.
422 with field-level errors. errors stored in .errors and .details.
429 Too Many Requests.
500 Internal Server Error.
501 Not Implemented.
502 Bad Gateway.
503 Service Unavailable.
Factory — creates the correct error class for any status code.
Returns true if err is an HttpError or has a statusCode. Type guard.
Serialize: { error, code, statusCode, details? }.

name: errorHandler([opts])
description: Configurable error-handling middleware. Formats error responses based on environment (dev vs production), includes stack traces in dev, hides internal details in production, and supports custom formatters and logging. Use with app.onError().

options.notes: Include stack traces in responses. Auto-detected from NODE_ENV.
Log errors to console.
Custom log function.
Custom response formatter: (err, req, isDev) => responseBody.
Callback on every error: (err, req, res) => void.

name: debug(namespace)
description: Lightweight namespaced debug logger with levels, colors, and timestamps. Enable via DEBUG env var or programmatically. Each namespace gets a unique color. Supports text and structured JSON output.

methods.description: Create a namespaced logger. Returns a function with level methods.
Log at debug level. Supports %s, %d, %j, %o format specifiers.
Log at trace level (most verbose).
Log at info level.
Log at warn level.
Log at error level.
Log at fatal level (most severe).
Set minimum log level globally.
Enable namespaces by pattern.
Disable all debug output.
Enable structured JSON output.
Toggle timestamps.
Toggle ANSI colors.
Set custom output stream.
Reset all settings to defaults.

options.notes: Comma-separated namespace patterns. Supports * glob and - prefix to exclude.
Minimum log level: trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal, silent.
